title: Directional indicator for protective face masks
abstract: An electronic directional indicator includes a microcontroller in electronic communication with an electronic compass, an electronic motion-detecting module and at least one light source that is controllably illuminable. A battery life of the electronic directional indicator is managed by monitoring the electronic motion-detecting module for motion within pre-defined time periods and reducing power to electronic components accordingly. The electronic directional indicator can be placed on the face shield portion of a self-contained breathing apparatus face mask to provide a constant source of orientation for firefighters in reduced- or zero-visibility environments
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A directional indicator, comprising: a microcontroller in signal communication with at least an electronic compass and an accelerometer; a plurality of individually-actuatable light sources arranged such that the position of each light source represents a unique compass direction, wherein each of said light sources is actuatable by said microcontroller to controllably emit light; at least one power source providing power to at least said microcontroller; and a shock sensor in signal communication with said accelerometer; wherein said shock sensor sends a wake-up signal to said accelerometer upon receiving a percussive shock when said directional indicator is in said powered-down, standby or sleep mode. 2. The directional indicator of claim 1, wherein said directional indicator is operable to toggle between a powered-down, standby or sleep mode and a full-power, operating mode. 3. The directional indicator of claim 2, wherein at least one of said microcontroller, said electronic compass and said accelerometer are powered off after a selected time interval of inactivity as measured by said accelerometer. 4. The directional indicator of claim 2, wherein each of said microcontroller, said electronic compass and said accelerometer are powered off after a selected time interval of inactivity as measured by said accelerometer. 5. The directional indicator of claim 1, wherein a tap by a userâs finger is a percussive shock suitable to cause said shock sensor to send said wake-up signal to said accelerometer. 6. The directional indicator of claim 1, wherein said wake-up signal causes said accelerometer to switch from said standby mode to said operating mode. 7. The directional indicator of claim 6, wherein said accelerometer remains in said operating mode for a preselected time interval after receiving said wake-up signal. 8. The directional indicator of claim 7, wherein, if movement of said directional indicator is measured by said accelerometer within said preselected time interval, said directional indicator toggles to said full-power, operating mode, wherein at least said microcontroller, said electronic compass and said accelerometer are operative. 9. The directional indicator of claim 8, wherein said movement is a predetermined confirmatory motion. 10. The directional indicator of claim 9, wherein said predetermined confirmatory motion is a nodding motion of the head of a user, when said directional indicator is attached to an implement worn on said head of said user. 11. The directional indicator of claim 10, wherein said implement is a facemask or face shield. 12. The directional indicator of claim 7, wherein, if no movement signals are generated by said accelerometer within said preselected time interval, said accelerometer is powered off and said directional indicator reverts to said standby mode. 13. The directional indicator of claim 7, wherein said preselected time interval is: between about 1 second and about 2 seconds; between about 1 second and about 4 seconds; or between about 1 second and about 10 seconds. 14. The directional indicator of claim 1, further comprising a rear housing portion and a front housing portion reversibly engaged to one another so as to encapsulate at least said microcontroller in a waterproof configuration. 15. The directional indicator of claim 14, wherein said front housing portion comprises an aperture for each light source, for allowing light emitted from said light sources to be visible from the exterior of said directional indicator. 16. The directional indicator of claim 14, wherein said front housing portion comprises a number of integral, transparent or translucent windows equal to the number of light sources for allowing light emitted from said light sources to be visible from the exterior of said directional indicator. 17. The directional indicator of claim 16, wherein each of said transparent or translucent windows is formed as a shape that indicates the unique compass direction associated with the light source immediately adjacent thereto. 18. The directional indicator of claim 1, wherein said microcontroller is configured to receive a heading direction signal from said electronic compass corresponding to the direction said directional indicator is pointing and activate at least one of said light sources corresponding to said heading direction. 19. A directional indicator, comprising: a microcontroller in signal communication with at least an electronic compass and an accelerometer; a display, in signal communication with said microcontroller, operable to show indicia corresponding to a heading direction of said directional indicator determined by said electronic compass; 

 at least one power source providing power to at least said microcontroller; and a shock sensor in signal communication with said accelerometer; wherein said shock sensor sends a wake-up signal to said accelerometer upon receiving a percussive shock when said directional indicator is in said standby mode. 20. The directional indicator of claim 19, wherein said directional indicator is operable to toggle between a low- or no-power, standby mode and a full-power, operating mode. 21. A directional indicator, comprising: a microcontroller in signal communication with at least an electronic compass and an accelerometer; a plurality of individually-actuatable light sources arranged such that the position of each light source represents a unique compass direction, wherein each of said light sources is actuatable by said microcontroller to controllably emit light; at least one power source providing power to at least said microcontroller; and a shock sensor in signal communication with said accelerometer; wherein at least one of said microcontroller, said electronic compass and said accelerometer are configured to enter a powered-off state after a predetermined amount of time after said accelerometer has not registered motion; wherein said shock sensor is configured to send a wake-up signal to said accelerometer after receiving a percussive shock sufficient to trigger said shock sensor, thereby causing said accelerometer to monitor for a predetermined confirmatory motion; and wherein said accelerometer is configured to recognize said predetermined confirmatory motion and: if recognized, causes said directional indicator to toggle from said powered-off state to a fully powered-on state; if not recognized, said accelerometer reverts to said powered-off state.
